Defendant Certainteed Corporations Notice Of Motion To Continue Trial Date, Keep Expert Discovery Open, And Re-Open Non-Expert Discovery For Limited Purposes
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Certainteed Corp.'s motion to continue trial date, keep expert discovery open, and re-open non-expert discovery for limited purposes.

APPEARANCES: Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; D. Paul Bird II, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP 267-4075 for deft & moving party Certainteed Corporation.

RULING: Hearing held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Certainteed Corp.'s motion to continue trial date, keep expert discovery open, and re-open non-expert discovery for limited purposes is granted. Defendant made an affirmative showing of good cause to continue the trial date, as required by CRC 3.1332, and there was no opposition to a trial continuance. The trial date is continued to April 14, 2014 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery will remain open and non-expert discovery is re-opened for the limited purpose of deposing witnesses with knowledge of the asbestos-cement pipe at issue. All other discovery that does not pertain to the asbestos-cement pipe issue remains closed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
